title: bryonia cretica subsp. marmorata
intro: Bryonia cretica subsp. marmorata is a subspecies of the Bryonia cretica plant, a type of flowering plant in the cucumber family (Cucurbitaceae). It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making it a popular remedy for respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and alleviating digestive issues. Bryonia cretica subsp. marmorata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
